mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching economic downturn, triggering risk-off positioning and equity sell-offs that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and moderating consumer price inflation indicates a healthy demand-supply balance that expands corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling window, or profit margin expansion does not correlate with equity outperformance during the specified macro regime.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its long-term trend level confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates into higher industrial sector earnings and lifts the broader S&P 500 index over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production prints above 100 yet the S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, indicating decoupling between manufacturing output and equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage debt outstanding reflects sustained household borrowing capacity and housing market liquidity, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Mortgage debt growth decouples from consumer spending data, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window despite rising credit conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation breaching the 262 index level signals entrenched price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite inflation crossing the threshold, or forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, invalidating the tightening valuation compression premise.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that boosts aggregate demand and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict equity direction in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances, or payroll growth diverges from unemployment trends without corresponding equity market reactions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,500 thousand units indicates strong builder confidence and downstream construction demand, which stimulates related industrial and financial sector earnings and lifts the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts remain elevated while the S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in more than half of the observations, indicating that construction activity no longer drives broader equity momentum.
